core_rule: Decisions that shape future work must survive the session where they were made.

decisions.title: GitHub Is The Source Of Truth
decisions.decision: GitHub (RodbotCC/CCAgent, branch main) is the durable source of truth for the project. The local file tree is the working copy.
decisions.context: The project needs continuity across agents, tools, MCP servers, local machines, and future sessions. Local-only state and chat history are not durable enough.
decisions.rationale: GitHub gives the project shared, versioned, inspectable memory. It lets other agents and tools connect through Git instead of relying on the user's local machine.
consequences: Important project memory belongs in repo files.
Ledgers must be committed.
Direct GitHub writes must consider unpushed local work.
The repo IS the memory of the build.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not move core continuity back into chat-only or local-only memory without explicit review.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit only if GitHub stops being accessible or another versioned source-of-truth system replaces it.

decisions.title: FileTree Orchestration Over RAG Memory
decisions.decision: The project uses FileTree orchestration as its primary memory and coordination model — not RAG / vector memory.
decisions.context: The user wants durable, inspectable, directory-aware continuity.
decisions.rationale: FileTree orchestration gives agents explicit, versioned, local context. Easier to audit, rebuild, hand off, and reason about than vague retrieval.
consequences: Important memory belongs in files.
Directories should become Boxes when meaningful.
Local context lives near files it governs.
Pieces.app stays as secondary memory backend, not primary.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not replace Box / local-file continuity with hidden memory without review.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit only if a hybrid retrieval layer is added as a helper while keeping file tree as source of truth.

decisions.title: Client Boxes Are Canonical Client Truth
decisions.decision: Auto/Client Boxes/<Name>/ is the canonical per-client memory and operating substrate. The Boxes page and orchestrator state are read / render / generation layers over that substrate.
decisions.context: There was confusion about whether the boxes UI/page or Auto/Boxes umbrella was the actual client truth.
decisions.rationale: Client-specific complexity should live inside the client's own Box. One client's constraints should not force global automation rewrites.
consequences: Client-specific edits happen in the relevant Client Box.
Boxes page is never canonical client truth.
Allowed-To-Know layer (PROB-001) will live inside each Client Box.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not make UI or generated dashboard state the canonical source for client truth without explicit migration.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit only if a database-backed canonical client layer is intentionally introduced.

decisions.title: Seven-Day Plans Are Strategy Drafts, Not Send Licenses
decisions.decision: A seven-day plan is a strategy draft. It does not authorize sending by itself. Reply received -> plan is stale.
decisions.context: Some plans were written before guardrails were fully enforced. The Brenda & Steve audit revealed plans with stale dates, risky commitment language, and enrichment leakage.
decisions.rationale: Plans age. Replies, transcripts, state sweeps, and updated guardrails can invalidate a previously good plan.
alternatives_considered: Treat plan text as send authority
Require manual rewrite before every move
consequences: Scheduled fires must treat plans as input, not authority.
If a lead replies, plan is stale.
Plan audits check for stale dates, risky commitments, enrichment leakage.
Brenda's plan now opens with Audit-cleaned line — that's the template.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not allow automation to send directly from plan text without guardrail validation.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit if a formal plan compiler / validator is built that enforces all gates before execution.

decisions.title: Risky Sales Moves Require Isolated Approval
decisions.decision: Risky sales moves may be suggested by automation, but they require explicit isolated approval before customer-facing send.
risky_move_examples: strong claims not grounded in comms
decisions.context: The Brenda & Steve fee-waiver move may have been strategically correct, but it was approved in a rushed batch context and did not receive enough isolated risk friction.
decisions.rationale: Good sales judgment and safe automation are not the same thing. A human should explicitly own financial, pricing, scope, or risky personalization commitments.
alternatives_considered: Allow risky moves inside normal batch approval
consequences: Risky moves should become explicit approval cards.
Approval cards should name the risk clearly.
Batch approval should not hide financial / scope commitments.
Approval UI specifics still under review (see decisions_needing_review).
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not let risky commitments ship as ordinary follow-up copy without explicit approval.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit after approval UI supports isolated risk confirmations robustly.

decisions.title: Boxes Page Is Display Layer, Not Canonical Truth
decisions.decision: The boxes route / page is a UI read / display surface over Client Boxes, Staff Boxes, and orchestrator state. It does not own canonical client truth.
decisions.context: The sitemap says the Boxes page reads Auto/Client Boxes, Auto/Staff Boxes, Auto/orchestrator/state, and exposes a single-surface dossier view. That could visually imply ownership if not documented.
decisions.rationale: UI should make truth visible, not become truth accidentally.
consequences: Fix client truth in Client Boxes.
Fix generated state through sources / generators.
Page changes update sitemap (DEC-007).
Boxes page should communicate source freshness.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not write client truth into the Boxes page layer without source-of-truth migration.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit if a database or app-native canonical Box store replaces file-based Client Boxes.

decisions.title: Page-Asset Sitemap Is Mandatory Done Gate For Page Changes
decisions.decider: Existing project policy / accepted into ledger system
decisions.decision: Any task that changes a page, route behavior, or page data binding is not complete until page_asset_sitemap.md is updated.
decisions.context: The Comeketo Page-Asset Source Of Truth already defines this mandatory Done Gate. Formalized into the ledger system 2026-04-28.
decisions.rationale: Page behavior and data ownership drift quickly if not recorded.
consequences: UI / page work includes sitemap update in same unit of work.
Asset / Widget Map links to sitemap rather than duplicating it.
Audits can fail page work if sitemap is stale.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not remove this Done Gate without replacing it with an equal or stronger page ownership system.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit only if Page Ledgers or an automated page map supersede the sitemap.

decisions.title: Build Ledgers One At A Time
decisions.decision: The ledger system is designed and implemented one ledger at a time. The user signals (typically by typing P) to proceed to the next.
decisions.context: The ledger system is large (~22 planned ledgers). Implementing it all at once would be too broad and would create shallow files.
decisions.rationale: One-ledger-at-a-time keeps focus, creates rhythm, and lets the user hand clean implementation outlines to the building agent.
consequences: Each ledger gets a dedicated outline at /Ledger Drafts/.
Implementation evolves without overwhelming repo.
Build order tracked in INDEX.md.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not attempt a massive all-ledgers rewrite unless the user explicitly changes workflow.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit after first wave of ledgers is implemented.

decisions.title: Triad Spine: Box + Ledger + Sub-agent
decisions.decision: The architectural spine of Comeketo Agent is the Box + Ledger + Sub-agent triad. Anything with state that learns or needs updating gets all three.

triad_legs.Box: the unit of state — Client Boxes, Staff Boxes, Coworker records, eventually any state-bearing entity
triad_legs.Ledger: the legible memory of that Box — project-wide ledgers under LEDGERS/; per-Box ledgers stamp from LEDGERS/LOCAL_TEMPLATE/BOX_LEDGER_TEMPLATE.md
triad_legs.Sub-agent: the operator that reads / updates the Box and its Ledger — canonical config in /Subagent Boxes/<name>/; runnable form in CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/

decisions.standing_rule: Legibility is more important than the build itself. Code without a corresponding Ledger entry is debt to repay. A Ledger reader should understand the system without opening code.
decisions.jake_exact_words: For everything that we build we cannot be lazy about the legibility. I am actually more concerned about the legibility than the build.
decisions.context: After the plumbing-first attempt was scrapped under demo pressure (see COMM-2026-04-28-008), the architectural spine wasn't formally named. On 2026-04-29 the triad was codified as the rule organizing everything stateful.
decisions.rationale: Ledgers without Boxes are floating memory. Boxes without Ledgers are illegible state. Sub-agents without either are operators with no memory. Skipping any leg produces a system that cannot continue itself across agents.
consequences: Before designing anything stateful, ask: Where is the Box? Where is the Ledger? Where will the Sub-agent live?
The triad is currently lopsided (more Boxes than Ledgers, more Ledgers than Sub-agents).
Per-Box ledgers stamp from LEDGERS/LOCAL_TEMPLATE/BOX_LEDGER_TEMPLATE.md.
Steward sub-agents follow the global_ledger_steward pattern.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not propose architectural patterns that bypass any leg of the triad. If a system has state, it gets all three.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit only if a fundamentally different state-management primitive emerges (would also trigger DEC-003 review).

decisions.title: Three-Phase Build Discipline (A -> B -> C)
decisions.decision: Build order, in this exact sequence: Phase A finish all Ledgers (currently here) -> Phase B finish all Sub-agents -> Phase C build Subagent Boxes.

A.name: Ledgers
A.description: Ingest outline drafts from /Ledger Drafts/ one at a time, in user-confirmed order.

B.name: Sub-agents
B.description: Graduate the draft sub-agent packages at /Subagent Boxes/ into runnable app agents under CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/.

C.name: Subagent Boxes
C.description: Build Subagent Boxes (boxes of ledgers controlled by sub-agent configurations). The triad becomes operational.

decisions.context: After the rebuild, the triad needed to be filled in a specific order to avoid producing partial systems.
decisions.rationale: Ledgers without sub-agents are static memory. Sub-agents without ledgers have nowhere to write. Subagent Boxes require both prior layers.
consequences: Confirm phase before proposing structure.
Phase A pattern: build the ledger only, note sub-agent home as placeholder.
PROB-016 blocked on Phase A + Phase B completion.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not skip phases. Do not parallelize without explicit user approval.

decisions.title: TCL/GL Update Discipline (Same Unit Of Work)
decisions.decision: Cold-starting agents read TCL and GL first. If those are stale, every agent that boots from them is stale. Therefore: whoever does the work also updates the orientation surface in the same unit of work — not after, not later.
required_updates_per_change: Update header Last updated dates on TCL and GL when applicable.
Append to TCL Section 3 Recent Meaningful Changes for any non-trivial change.
Update TCL Section 1 Current Snapshot if anything structural changed.
Update GL Section 12 Recently Changed for major moves.
Update GL Section 2 Current World State if a structural fact changed.
Append to CCAgentindex/_ledger/activity.jsonl for any non-trivial delegation.
decisions.context: Failure mode happened during a Cowork -> Claude Code -> Cowork handoff on 2026-04-29: a Cowork session read a TCL written ~24 hours earlier and had no idea about that day's catch-up work.
decisions.jake_exact_words: The ledger is more important than the actual work. Literally everything needs to be ledgered.
decisions.rationale: The fix is not smarter agents — it's discipline. Orientation surfaces ARE the agent. If they're stale, the agent is stale.
consequences: Every non-trivial work session ends with a TCL/GL update.
Header dates are bumped accurately and frequently.
Cold-starting agents can trust TCL/GL as current.
global_ledger_steward verifies drift but does not replace this discipline.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not adopt patterns that defer ledger updates to 'later' or to a separate commit. Same unit of work, every time.
decisions.review_trigger: Does not expire. This is the meta-rule that keeps every other ledger usable.

decisions.title: Audit Ledger Removed From Build Queue
decisions.decision: The Audit Ledger is removed from the build queue. Audit-shaped findings will land in Open Problems, Decisions, Communications, or per-Box ledgers as appropriate.
decisions.context: After Communications Ledger landed and OPL had been live for a day, Jake observed that audit findings naturally distribute across the three sibling ledgers + per-Box ledgers. A dedicated Audit Ledger would duplicate coverage already provided.
decisions.jake_exact_words: I don't think we're ever going to need that. It's basically covered in the other things.
decisions.rationale: Standalone Audit Ledger would create three failure modes: coverage overlap, discoverability cost, maintenance overhead.
alternatives_considered: Build a thin Audit Ledger that cross-references
consequences: INDEX.md row marked out-of-scope (2026-04-29).
GLOBAL_LEDGER Section 8 Audit row marked similarly.
Build order item 4 (in INDEX) struck through.
Audit-pattern findings route to Open Problems / Decisions / Communications / per-Box.
Brenda audit pattern continues to work — routes findings into existing ledgers.
Draft outline at /Ledger Drafts/# Audit Ledger.txt stays as reference; not deleted.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Confidence is medium because this decision is hours old. If a future agent finds audit findings genuinely don't fit any of the four homes, it's worth re-opening this question. Don't reverse without that evidence.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit if a future audit pattern doesn't fit Open Problems / Decisions / Communications / per-Box; or if audit-shaped reporting becomes a frequent operator-facing need (compliance, regulatory); or if volume makes per-ledger distribution painful.

decisions.title: Box Reports Are Synthesized Views, Not Copies
decisions.decision: A Box Report is a synthesized view over Auto/Client Boxes/<Name>/. The server reads the box folder on every /api/reports/get call and assembles a documents[] payload from the live files. No report.json is written into the box folder. Q&A history lives at CCAgentindex/reports/_box_conversations/<box_id>.jsonl.
decisions.context: The 2026-04-27 manual brenda_and_steve workspace already proved the pattern by accident — its documents were a hand-curated copy of Brenda's box files. Phase 1 of the Intake → Box unification asked: real report.json mirror, or rebuild on read?
decisions.rationale: A copy creates a drift surface — two records of the same content diverging silently. Synthesis has no drift surface. Triad-aligned (DEC-2026-04-29-001): the Box is the unit of state; an Intake-side report cannot be a parallel Box.
alternatives_considered: Persisted mirror inside each box folder — rejected (drift)
Persisted mirror outside the box under CCAgentindex/reports/<box_id>/ — rejected (same drift; pollutes workspace list)
Hybrid mirror with mtime invalidation — rejected for Phase 1 (extra moving parts before measurement justifies them)
consequences: Box-Report ingest writes into Auto/Client Boxes/<Name>/intake_drops/<file>
Box-Report doc deletion from Intake is forbidden (HTTP 405)
Auto/ symlink is written through only for intake_drops/; documented as exception in page_asset_sitemap.md
Future analytics/page/agent-as-box work should default to the same posture
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not introduce a persisted Box Report mirror without first measuring read cost and demonstrating a cache strategy. Drift is the failure mode; performance has not been the failure mode.
decisions.review_trigger: Synthesis latency materially affects Intake page loads; or a future architecture requires a stable identifier for Box Report content that can't be derived on read.

decisions.title: One Identity Per Box Across The App (slug = box id)
decisions.decision: A Box Report's slug is the box id from /api/boxes/list, with no synthetic prefix. The earlier draft's client_box__ prefix was dropped because every existing client-box id is already unique within the catalog. Workspace slugs that collide with a Client Box id are refused at create (HTTP 409).
decisions.context: Cross-page navigation between Intake and Boxes needs to refer to the same entity by the same name. Two identities would force a translation table on every link, every menu item, and every ledger reference.
decisions.rationale: One identity = trivial cross-nav (go.push('intake', { openSlug: id }) and go.push('boxes', { selectId: id }) both work because the slug is the id). Future ledger entries that mention a box stay stable across surfaces.
alternatives_considered: client_box__<id> prefix (the original draft) — rejected (redundant; lookup already resolves uniquely)
Separate Box-Report slug minted at creation — rejected (translation table; doubles the namespace)
consequences: POST /api/reports/create rejects any workspace slug that matches a Client Box id (HTTP 409)
All 5 existing workspaces are safe (no collision)
Box rename = id change = orphans the corresponding _box_conversations/<old_id>.jsonl; acceptable for Phase 1
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Adding a translation layer between box ids and report slugs would force every cross-surface reference to be rewritten. Don't introduce a separate identity space without a concrete need.
decisions.review_trigger: A Client Box ever needs to project multiple report views (e.g., 'lite' vs 'full'); or a privacy/access-control requirement makes the box id unsafe to expose at the URL layer.

decisions.title: Box Reports Refresh On Read
decisions.decision: Every /api/reports/get?slug=<box_id> and /api/reports/list call re-reads the live Client Box folder. No cache, no debounce, no invalidation flag. Workspaces keep using their stored report.json.
decisions.context: DEC-2026-04-29-005 left freshness open: re-read or cache. Phase 1 picks the simplest answer that avoids stale-cache bugs.
decisions.rationale: Cheap (~28 boxes × ~10 files = sub-100ms). Correct (no stale-cache bug class). Reversible (a 5s TTL cache later is a single-function change with no schema migration).
alternatives_considered: mtime-based cache — rejected for Phase 1 (small win, more code paths)
Persistent cache file — rejected (same drift problem 005 is trying to avoid)
consequences: Box Reports always reflect what's in the box folder right now
If list-endpoint latency becomes a UX problem, the agreed remediation is in-memory TTL cache, not persisted state
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Don't add caching speculatively. Measure first.
decisions.review_trigger: Intake page load time becomes user-visibly slow on production; or Client Box count grows past ~200.

decisions.title: Phase 1 Intake → Box Is Read-Mostly (no agent-config plumbing yet)
decisions.decision: Phase 1 ships read-only synthesis, the Reports list split, and box-folder ingest. It does NOT plumb each box's AGENTS.md into the /api/reports/<slug>/ask system prompt, build per-box analytics templates, or start any reactive-box-bus / ledger trickle-down work.
decisions.context: The bigger architecture (every state-bearing surface becomes a box; ledgers route entries downstream to subscribed boxes; sub-agents per box interpret incoming entries) is the right ultimate frame and the hardest piece in the project. Building it before the ledger system and sub-agent fleet are stable would force re-wiring on every new ledger and every new sub-agent.
decisions.rationale: Cleanup-phase rule (DEC-2026-04-28-008): one ledger at a time. Same posture for architecture: one major surface at a time. Phase 1 surfaces have zero dependencies on agent-config plumbing or the bus and ship cleanly today. Phase 2 (per-box AGENTS.md) can ship later without disturbing Phase 1.
alternatives_considered: Ship Phase 1 + agent-config plumbing together — rejected (stretches scope; risks the today-value)
Ship Phase 1 + analytics-as-box — rejected (analytics templates need ledger system further along)
Defer Phase 1 entirely until the bus is ready — rejected (forfeits today's value)
consequences: Intake ask on a Box Report uses the same generic prompt assembly as Workspaces
Hugo's existing AGENTS.md is read as a document but not honored as an operating contract — yet
Phase 2 scope: read AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / 06_logic.md / 07_skills_used.md / 08_automations.md when present; fold into ask system prompt; fall back to generic client-box assistant prompt
Reactive box-bus / ledger trickle-down deferred until ledger taxonomy and stewards are stable; lands as its own Decisions Ledger entry when shipped
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not start Phase 2/3 work or the bus design while Phase A (ledgers) of DEC-2026-04-29-002 is in flight. The phasing rule is the protection against half-finished architecture.
decisions.review_trigger: Phase A of DEC-2026-04-29-002 completes (planned ledgers active, stewards landed); or a user-facing need requires box-aware ask before then.

decisions.title: Reactive Box Network Is The Target Architecture (schema only; runtime deferred to Phase C)
decisions.decider: User (Jake) — autonomous-call delegated to Cowork session
affected_systems: every stateful entity in the project
every ledger authored from this point forward
every Box authored from this point forward
decisions.narrows: DEC-2026-04-29-008 — carves the schema seam out of the deferred work while preserving the runtime deferral
decisions.decision: The project's target architecture is a reactive network of Boxes connected by a routed Ledger bus. The architecture is specified now (Phase 0, this session) in BOX_BUS_LEDGER.md. The runtime — router daemon (or post-write hook), propagation ledger, T1/T2/T3 interpreters, validation enforcement, cycle enforcement — is deferred to Phase C of DEC-2026-04-29-002. Phase 0 ships schema and rules only: box.json manifest schema, ledger envelope schema, three routing tiers (Global / Domain / Local), three interpreter tiers (T1 deterministic / T2 small-LLM / T3 full sub-agent), hard-refusal cycle policy, what-is-a-Box binding rule, and two worked examples (Hugo Casillas Client Box + Analytics Source Channels Snapshot Box).
decisions.context: Architecture first sketched in chat during 2026-04-29 Phase 1 Intake → Box session and intentionally deferred (DEC-2026-04-29-008) because building runtime before ledger taxonomy and sub-agent fleet stabilize would force re-wiring on every new ledger and every new sub-agent. Today's update: Phase 9 (Definition of Done) closed the most foundational completion-rules gap; 9 ledgers are now active out of ~19 planned. Without a written architectural target, every remaining ledger risks being authored without the bus shape in mind — creating migration debt at Phase C. Writing the schema down now (a Phase A activity — markdown ledger) gives every future ledger and Box a target. Writing runtime would still violate DEC-2026-04-29-008 and is held.
rationale: Schema first / routing second / interpreters third / runtime last. The hard-but-cheap part is the schema.

Triad spine compatibility (DEC-2026-04-29-001): Box Ledger owns the concept; Box Bus Ledger owns the wire shape; Definition of Done owns the completion gate; Decisions Ledger owns the architectural lock.
Migration debt avoidance: every ledger and Box authored between today and Phase C ships envelope-aware. When runtime lands, no migration pass required.
Anti-half-finished discipline: this Decision narrows DEC-2026-04-29-008 by carving the schema seam out of deferred work while preserving runtime deferral.
Auditability: a written architecture is reviewable by every future agent. Chat-history architecture is not.
alternatives_considered: Defer schema along with runtime — rejected: every future ledger and Box would be authored guessing at a target.
Ship schema + T1-only runtime now — rejected: re-touching cost is exactly what DEC-2026-04-29-008 prevents.
Wait until Phase C and author schema + runtime together — rejected: ~19 ledgers and ~40 Boxes would need migration.
Author schema in chat / docs / a Decisions Ledger row only — rejected: design is large enough to need its own ledger with worked examples and visuals.
Different name (Event Bus Ledger, Subscription Network Ledger) — picked Box Bus Ledger because the unit of state on both ends is a Box.
consequences: Phase A continues with envelope-aware ledgers — every remaining Phase A ledger declares its tier at authoring.
Box Ledger gets cross-reference: concept lives there, manifest shape lives in Box Bus Ledger.
Definition of Done §5.7 gains manifest-shape compliance check — soft today (no runtime), hard at Phase C.
No runtime work permitted in Phase A — router, propagation ledger, interpreters, validation, cycle enforcement all deferred to Phase C.
Phase B steward agents author manifests for their target ledgers/systems as part of graduation.
Phase C+1 backfill authors manifests for 28 Client Boxes + 10 Staff Boxes + orchestrator + inbox-skill + ledger directory + app-ui root.
Open Problems Ledger absorbs any pre-Phase-C architectural risks.
analytics-snapshot Box kind reserved for Phase C+1 when each analytics script becomes a Box.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not ship runtime (router, propagation ledger, interpreters, validation enforcement) during Phase A. The schema-only seam is the entire point. If runtime feels tempting, route the urge into Open Problems or Communications — not into code. DEC-2026-04-29-008's Do Not Undo Casually still binds against runtime. Do not change the manifest schema, envelope schema, tier model, or cycle policy without an explicit Decisions Ledger update.
review_trigger: Phase A completes (all planned ledgers active, stewards landed for load-bearing ones) — unblocks Phase B + Phase C runtime work.
A foreseen-but-unspecified field is needed in box.json or envelope — review whether new Decision or BOX_BUS_LEDGER update is enough.
A new tier proposed beyond Global / Domain / Local — Decision-level change.
A new interpreter tier proposed beyond T1 / T2 / T3 — Decision-level change.
Cycle policy challenged (real use case for declared cycles) — currently hard-refusal; would be Decision-level change.

history.event: created. Phase 0 schema shipped same day at BOX_BUS_LEDGER.md + BOX_BUS_LEDGER.json + three Mermaid visuals (box_bus_topology, interpreter_tiers, envelope_routing_flow). Box Ledger and Definition of Done updated to point at it. Companion Communications entry: COMM-2026-04-29-007.

decisions.title: Phase A Complete — Ledger Buildout Done
decisions.decider: User (Jake) — autonomous-call delegated to Cowork session ('let's just bang it all out')
affected_systems: Phase C Reactive Box Network runtime (still deferred per DEC-2026-04-29-013)
related_ledgers: DEC-2026-04-29-002 (three-phase build — this completes Phase A of that)
DEC-2026-04-29-013 (Reactive Box Network — runtime still deferred to Phase C)
decisions.decision: Phase A (Ledgers) is complete as of 2026-04-29. 17 ledgers active across global + domain tiers. All exit criteria in PHASE.md §3 met. Phase B (Sub-agents) is now unblocked. Phase C runtime stays deferred per DEC-2026-04-29-013.
decisions.context: Phase A started 2026-04-28 with the first 5 Phases (Global, TCL, North Star, File Directory, Open Problems). On 2026-04-29 the project shipped Phases 6-21 in a single sustained sprint: Communications, Decisions, Box Ledger, DoD, Box Bus, Source-of-Truth, Connections, File Contents, Asset/Widget Map, Settings, 5 Page Ledgers (boxes, intake, automation, delegations, settings page), and the Phase Ledger that closes Phase A.
rationale: Every architectural pattern future work depends on is now named and written down.
Envelope-aware authoring rule (DEC-2026-04-29-013) has 6 real precedents — every future ledger has a target.
The 9 lower-risk Page Ledgers + Widget Ledgers + Prompt/Reconstruction + Scout are explicitly deferred — their absence does not block exit (per PHASE.md §3 Deferred section).
Honest accounting given to user during the sprint: the load-bearing work was the high-risk wave + Phase Ledger; everything beyond that is more of the same patterns.
Phasing rule (DEC-2026-04-29-002) protects against half-finished architecture. Phase B work cannot start until Phase A closes.
alternatives_considered: Continue Phase A indefinitely until all 14 Page Ledgers + Widget + Prompt/Recon + Scout authored — rejected: hits diminishing returns; user explicitly noted 'grip is tightening'; better to land Phase B with real load instead of dragging Phase A.

Declare Phase A complete after 15 project-level ledgers without any Page Ledgers — rejected: high-risk pages (boxes, intake, automation, delegations, settings) carry too much architectural rationale to defer to Phase B.
consequences: Phase B opens. The 5 draft sub-agent packages at /Subagent Boxes/ become eligible for graduation.
Authoring more Page Ledgers becomes Phase B/C work as needed (not a blocker).
Widget Ledgers, Prompt/Reconstruction, Scout, per-directory Box.md stamps remain explicitly deferred.
The 'don't leak Phase A' rules from PHASE.md §3 sunset; the 'don't leak Phase B' rules in PHASE.md §4 take effect.
Phase C runtime work (router, propagation ledger, T1/T2/T3 interpreters) continues to be deferred per DEC-2026-04-29-013.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not retroactively reopen Phase A to author lower-risk Page Ledgers or Widget Ledgers. Those are Phase B/C work. The phasing rule (DEC-2026-04-29-002) is the protection; reopening Phase A would erode it.
review_trigger: Phase B exit criteria are met → declare Phase B complete in a similar Decision
A real architectural gap is found that requires authoring a top-level ledger that wasn't covered → may warrant a 'Phase A reopened for X' Decision
User explicitly wants to defer Phase B and reorder phases — would require a new architectural Decision

history.event: created. Phase A officially declared complete as of this Decision. PHASE.md and PHASE.json mark current phase status accordingly.

decisions.title: Unified Ledger Box Pattern — LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/
decisions.decider: User (Jake) — 'compile these together so that ledgers and boxes and sub-agents are all one thing'
affected_systems: the 5 draft sub-agent packages at /Subagent Boxes/
CCAgentindex/agents/global_ledger_steward/ (existing runnable form — stays for now; future Phase B+1 reorg)
related_ledgers: PHASE.md §4 (Phase B target — refined by this Decision)
DEC-2026-04-29-001 (Triad Spine — this Decision realizes the structural side of the triad)
DEC-2026-04-29-013 (Reactive Box Network runtime deferred — this Decision adds structural unification without violating runtime deferral)
DEC-2026-04-29-014 (Phase A Complete — this Decision is the first Phase B work that builds on Phase A's foundation)
decisions.decision: Each project-level ledger gets a unified Box at `LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/` containing: `box.json` (manifest per BOX_BUS §2.1), `BOX.md` (per-Box orientation per BOX_LEDGER), `steward/{AGENTS.md, config.json, prompt.md}` (the sub-agent package), and `receipts/` (run receipts). The ledger files themselves (e.g., `TEMPORAL_CONTINUITY.md`) STAY at `LEDGERS/` and are governed by the Box via `box.json.owns[]` path reference — never moved. This is the Phase B target layout that replaces the prior framing of 'graduate to CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/'.
decisions.context: Phase A landed 16 project-level ledgers. The 5 draft sub-agent packages at /Subagent Boxes/ (file_directory, global_ledger, north_star, open_problems, temporal_continuity) all explicitly named `LEDGERS/AGENTS/<name>_steward/` as their target path — each scattering steward configs separately from the ledgers they govern. Jake's question on Phase B opening: 'compile these together so that ledgers and boxes and sub-agents are all one thing.' The triad spine (DEC-2026-04-29-001) names Box + Ledger + Sub-agent as the architectural unit; the structural realization is one folder per ledger that contains all three.
rationale: Triad realization — Box + Ledger + Sub-agent live in one folder per ledger; navigating to /LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/ shows the operator everything that governs that ledger.

No move = no ripple — ledger files (GLOBAL_LEDGER.md, TEMPORAL_CONTINUITY.md, etc.) stay in place. The Read-First protocol (referenced in CLAUDE.md, every cross-ledger link, every agent bootstrap) is unchanged.
Manifest-first — every Box ships a box.json that satisfies BOX_BUS §2.1. When Phase C runtime lands, the router has manifests to consume.
Receipts in-Box — run outputs land in receipts/ inside the Box, not scattered in CCAgentindex/_ledger/<name>_steward_runs/. Easier to find; one folder is the full picture.
Phase B clarity — the prior framing ('graduate to CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/') was correct but scattered. The unified Box pattern is more legible AND more aligned with the eventual Phase C runtime.
alternatives_considered: Move the ledger files into LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/ — REJECTED. Massive cascading update of every CLAUDE.md / cross-ledger link / agent bootstrap. The Read-First protocol breaks.

Keep current layout (LEDGERS/AGENTS/<name>/ + CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/) and just stamp a manifest at LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>.json — REJECTED. Loses the unification Jake explicitly asked for; doesn't co-locate the Box-shaped folder structure.
Symlink LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/ledger.md to LEDGERS/<NAME>.md — REJECTED. Symlinks don't survive git well across platforms; introduces a needed-file-not-found failure mode.
consequences: Phase B target refines from 'graduate to CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/' to 'graduate to LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/steward/'.
Each ledger Box contains 3 files in steward/ + 1 box.json + 1 BOX.md + receipts/ = 5 files + 1 directory minimum per Box.
16 project-level ledgers × the unified pattern = 16 ledger Boxes when Phase B graduations complete.
Existing LEDGERS/AGENTS/global_ledger_steward/ becomes LEDGERS/BOXES/global_ledger/steward/ (reorg, not recreation).

Existing CCAgentindex/agents/global_ledger_steward/ stays as the runnable form for now; future reorg may consolidate but is NOT required for Phase B exit. The Phase C runtime can decide later whether to read the runnable form from the Box directly or maintain the CCAgentindex/agents/ pattern.
Drafts at /Subagent Boxes/ remain in place as starting material — the unified Box copies, doesn't move. Drafts can be deleted after their corresponding Box is built and verified.
PHASE.md §4 must be updated to name the unified Box pattern as the Phase B target.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not move ledger files (GLOBAL_LEDGER.md, etc.) into the LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/ folders. The whole point of this Decision is that the ledger files stay where they are and the Box governs them by path reference. Moving would break the Read-First protocol and require a sweeping cross-ledger / cross-CLAUDE.md / cross-agent-bootstrap update.
review_trigger: Phase C runtime architecture takes a different path than BOX_BUS §2.1 anticipates — would warrant a Decisions Ledger update
A real operator workflow makes one folder structure noticeably worse than the other in practice — pattern can be revised
Consolidation between LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/steward/ and CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/ becomes load-bearing for Phase C runtime — review at that boundary

history.event: created. First unified Box stamped at LEDGERS/BOXES/temporal_continuity/ as the proof of pattern. Companion Communications entry: COMM-2026-04-29-016.

decisions.title: Deprecation Ledger Lives At Global Tier; Snapshot Protocol Pairs With It
affected_systems: every ledger that has rows superseded
decisions.decision: The Deprecation Ledger lives at the global tier as LEDGERS/DEPRECATION.md + LEDGERS/DEPRECATION.json. It is paired with a Snapshot Protocol (Deprecation Ledger §7) that defines what is captured, on what cadence, where it lives, retention rules, recovery procedure, and health checks. Tier global. Snapshots zip into _snapshots/<cadence>/ at project root, gitignored. Cardinal rule: nothing leaves the project without a Deprecation entry and a Snapshot reference.

alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Scatters audit trail. Future agents would need to walk every Box to know what's been retired project-wide. Recovery is hard because there's no central pointer to snapshots. Cross-cutting questions become impossible to answer without a global view.

alternatives_considered.alternative: No deprecation ledger; rely on git history alone
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Git tells when a file was deleted but not why, what replaced it, what depends on it that's still broken, or whether it's recoverable from a snapshot. Git is content-truth; deprecation needs metadata-truth.
Snapshots are the recovery surface for deprecation; they have no independent purpose. Splitting creates coordination burden ('did I update both?') and doesn't earn its complexity.
Bedrock is for canonical app state. Snapshots are local-only recovery archives that should not push to GitHub. They live at project root in _snapshots/.

alternatives_considered.alternative: Defer entire ledger to Phase C with the runtime
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Phase B starting now; already accumulating retirement debt (four backfill entries are real). Authoring schema + protocol now matches DEC-2026-04-29-013 (schema canonical, runtime deferred — no migration debt later).

consequences: Single source of truth for retirements — anyone asking 'what was deprecated and why' reads one ledger
Recovery is always answerable — every entry pairs with snapshot_id + snapshot_path (or explicit not_recoverable reason)
No silent deletion — cardinal rule binds every agent
Phase A backfill in scope — four pre-existing retirements captured immediately
Snapshot Protocol gives operational shape to 'save before delete' — LEDGERS/scripts/snapshot.sh invokable manually today; cron-wired in Phase C
_snapshots/ gitignored — local recovery only; GitHub stays source of truth for live content
Pairs with the unified Box pattern — Phase B will land LEDGERS/BOXES/deprecation/ to match temporal_continuity/
Cross-ledger coordination is explicit — Deprecation entries cross-reference Decisions / Open Problems / Communications; affected domain ledgers drop their rows when something deprecates
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Don't move the Deprecation Ledger to a per-Box scheme without a Decision — the cross-cutting view is the point. Don't push _snapshots/ to GitHub. Don't allow retirement without an entry. Don't promote past 'deprecated' without the §5 incoming-link audit.
review_trigger: Ledger grows past a few hundred active entries and search/UX becomes painful
Snapshot disk pressure becomes meaningful
Phase C Box Bus runtime ships and we want to evaluate whether per-Box emits[] obviates human-authored entries
Regulatory or compliance requirement appears mandating a different retention regime

history.event: created. Authored alongside LEDGERS/DEPRECATION.md + LEDGERS/DEPRECATION.json + LEDGERS/scripts/snapshot.sh + _snapshots/ directory + .gitignore update. Four backfill entries seeded (DEPR-2026-04-30-001..004). Triggered by Jake's observation that the project tree was accumulating retirement debt without a recovery mechanism.

decisions.title: Atom Ledger: PROBs Decompose 1:N Into Single-Session Claimable Atoms (Global Tier)
affected_systems: every active PROB (13 PROBs become 13×N atoms once decomposed)
every agent claiming work (Cowork sessions, Claude Code subprocesses, Codex sessions, future runnable stewards)
future Atoms UI page or panel
decisions.decision: The Atom Ledger lives at global tier as LEDGERS/ATOMS.md + LEDGERS/ATOMS.json. PROBs decompose 1:N into atoms via mandatory parent_problem_id linkage. Single-writer claim protocol enables parallel agent work. Granularity rule caps atoms at 4h estimated effort; bigger gets re-decomposed. Acceptance criteria must be concrete, verifiable, single-pass. Phase A: manual decomposition. Phase B: Atomizer Steward. Phase C: atoms as Box Bus envelope citizens.

alternatives_considered.alternative: Atoms inline inside each PROB record (extending OPL with atoms[])
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Open Problems is a state-of-brokenness ledger; bloating each entry with claim metadata muddles its purpose. PROB entries grow unbounded as atoms accumulate. Cross-PROB atom queries become parsing-heavy.
Atoms cross-cut PROBs and PROBs cross-cut Boxes. Per-Box scatter loses the dependency graph and makes parallel work harder. Cross-Box claim collisions harder to detect.

alternatives_considered.alternative: No new ledger; just TaskCreate-style task list
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Tasks are session-scoped; atoms must persist across sessions and agents. The architectural shape (durable, claimable, dependency-aware) requires a ledger.

alternatives_considered.alternative: Defer to Phase C with Box Bus runtime
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Same reasoning as DEC-2026-04-30-002. Schema works without runtime; authoring now = zero migration debt; already accumulating frozen PROBs.

consequences: Parallel agent work becomes possible — multiple agents claim distinct atoms without collision
Monolithic PROBs stop scaring agents — PROB-016 was deferred 2 days; decomposed, it's 26 single-session claims
Acceptance criteria force concrete work — 'Improve X' stops being valid
Audit trail compounds — atom_completed events land in _ledger/activity.jsonl
Atomizer Steward (Phase B) scope crystallizes
Phase C runtime gets clean target — atoms as first-class Box Bus envelope citizens
Pairs with unified Box pattern — Phase B lands LEDGERS/BOXES/atoms/
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Don't move atoms inline into PROBs — cross-cutting view enables parallel work. Don't break parent_problem_id requirement — orphan atoms drift back into 'tasks'. Don't loosen 4h granularity rule. Don't bypass claim protocol. Don't auto-close PROBs when atoms complete — PROB closure is separate review.
review_trigger: Ledger grows past a few thousand entries and search/UX painful
24h stale-claim rule proves too short or too long in practice
Phase C runtime ships and real-time atom dispatch evaluation
Regulatory/compliance audit-shape requirement appears

history.event: created. Authored alongside LEDGERS/ATOMS.md + LEDGERS/ATOMS.json. PROB-2026-04-28-016 decomposed as proof (26 atoms, ~21.5 estimated hours). Triggered by Jake's observation that monolithic PROBs freeze the project: 'if we have an atomized, decomposed list of all the stuff that needs to be done, then there'll be hundreds of easy problems instead of massive open problems.'

decisions.title: Canonical Steward Path = LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/steward/ (resolves legacy vs unified-Box fork)
decisions.decider: Claude (Cowork) under Jake's 'best move you call it' delegation
affected_systems: every ledger steward sub-agent (current: global_ledger_steward legacy + temporal_continuity_steward unified + atomizer_steward unified declarative)
the 4 unpromoted draft stewards (file_directory, north_star, open_problems, plus temporal_continuity runnable form)
decisions.decision: The canonical home for every ledger steward sub-agent is LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/steward/. Files: AGENTS.md (declarative), config.json (operating config), prompt.md (dispatch). Receipts at LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/receipts/. LEDGERS/AGENTS/<name>/ stays as a thin canonical-pointer/symlink directory. CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/ is legacy; survives only for global_ledger_steward until ATOM-2026-04-30-0044 migrates it. server.py _agent_run gets a fallback chain: legacy path first, then unified Box path. Dispatch URL stays POST /api/agents/<name>_steward/run.

alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Splits the unified Box pattern. BOX.md + box.json at LEDGERS/BOXES/, runnable bits elsewhere. Future agents look in two places. Defeats DEC-015.

alternatives_considered.alternative: Hybrid — symlinks from CCAgentindex/agents/ to unified Box (Path C)
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Symlink discipline is a maintenance burden; prone to drift; second source of truth question. Server fallback chain is cleaner.
ATOM-0027 blocks 16 atoms. Indefinite deferral undermines the atomization architecture (queue with permanent blockers = freeze response).

consequences: DEC-2026-04-29-015 (unified Box) is now fully load-bearing for stewards
server.py _agent_run gets a small fallback-chain change (one line + smoke test) — lands as part of ATOM-0044
global_ledger_steward requires migration (ATOM-0044) — independent of the 4 promotion chains; can claim in parallel
The 4 unpromoted stewards use unified Box from the start — no legacy entanglement; ATOMS-0028..0043 unblocked
LEDGERS/AGENTS/<name>/ becomes thin canonical-pointer dir or is removed entirely (future decision)
CCAgentindex/ bedrock returns to app-state-only after migration completes — non-steward sub-agents (andre_escalation_ladder, inbox_triage) stay there
PROB-005 close-criteria simplify
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Don't reverse without an explicit DEC. Don't break global_ledger_steward during migration — keep legacy path working until smoke test passes. Don't introduce a third path. Don't put non-steward sub-agents into LEDGERS/BOXES/ — only ledger stewards belong there.
review_trigger: Fallback chain in _agent_run causes operational confusion (e.g., dual-path steward picked incorrectly)
Non-ledger sub-agent canonical-home question arises
Phase C Box Bus runtime ships — unified Box path becomes load-bearing for subscribes/emits

history.event: created. Resolved ATOM-2026-04-30-0027 (architectural-gate blocking 16 promotion atoms in PROB-005). Companion follow-on ATOM-2026-04-30-0044 authored for global_ledger_steward migration. First atom claimed-and-completed under the new claim protocol — proves the protocol works end-to-end.

decisions.title: Box = Ledger + Rules + Sub-agent + Config + Receipts (Box Network Architecture target primitive)
affected_systems: every Box project-wide (6 unified ledger Boxes + 28 Client + 10 Staff + planned page/automation Boxes)
every sub-agent (5 ledger stewards runnable + 2 operational sub-agents)
every CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md across the project
the entire bedrock structure under CCAgentindex/
decisions.decision: The mature primitive of the Comeketo Agent project is the fused Box: Box = Ledger + Rules + Sub-agent + Config + Receipts. Every Box is a stateful memory object with an operating contract. Mature shape includes BOX.md + box.json + LEDGER files (when applicable) + AGENTS.md (when behavior differs) + steward/ + receipts/ + inbox/outbox per Q5. Authority tiers structural: Tier 0 Constitutional / Tier 1 Coordination / Tier 2 Domain / Tier 3 Leaf. Trickle-down is filtered inheritance via interpreters (T1 deterministic ~80%, T2 small-LLM ~15%, T3 sub-agent ~5%). Box graph declared: every box.json has subscribes[] + emits[]. Every flow is Source → Interpreter → Destination with a receipt.

alternatives_considered.alternative: Path A: Keep separate (triad as loose neighbors) — DEC-2026-04-29-001 baseline
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Triad spine named the three pieces but left them structurally split. Two days of unified Box pattern at ledger tier proved fused primitive is materially more legible. Splitting forces every agent to remember which folder holds which piece.

alternatives_considered.alternative: Path B: Fuse only stewards (Box + sub-agent only; ledger files stay separate)
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Works for ledger Boxes (one canonical file) but not for multi-file Boxes (Client Boxes have 5+ files). The fused primitive treats the Box itself as the unit of state.

alternatives_considered.alternative: Path C: Defer to Phase C runtime (architecture-then-build)
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Scaffold proves architecture is half-written. Authoring lock now means agents author Boxes IN the fused shape, not against an inferior pattern that has to migrate later. DEC-013 defers RUNTIME, schema is canonical now.

alternatives_considered.alternative: Path D: Adopt scaffold without further architectural commitment
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Scaffold is synthesis/proposal per its own framing. 8 Open Questions sit unresolved. Adopting without locking the central commitment leaves architecture conditional. This DEC locks the fusion thesis; Q1-Q8 land separately under it.

consequences: Phase 1-8 build path activates — all 58 atoms under PROB-015 become live work
BOX_LEDGER.md gains mature Box shape (Phase 1.1 / ATOM-0056)
BOX_BUS_LEDGER.md gains source/interpreter/destination model (Phase 1.2 / ATOM-0057)
DEFINITION_OF_DONE.md gains Box-completion gate with 6 sub-gates (Phase 1.3 / ATOM-0058)
SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md gains authority-tier framing (Phase 1.4 / ATOM-0059)
LOCAL_TEMPLATE updates + Box scaffold script ships (Phase 2)
Every existing ledger gains a unified Box (Phase 3)
Every Box's box.json declares its graph membership (Phase 4)
Three real interpreters land — Atomizer Steward T3 (per Q4), Allowed-To-Know T3, Page-Asset T2 (Phase 5)
Box Bus runtime ships with first cross-Box envelope delivery (Phase 6)
Hugo Casillas Client Box first migration (Phase 7 per Q6)
Self-maintaining system — drift monitors, sweepers, validators (Phase 8)
Every CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md eventually updates per Q8
Future PROBs decompose automatically via Atomizer Steward
Parallel-work substrate compounds beyond ledger tier
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Don't reverse without an explicit superseding DEC. Don't fragment the fused primitive. Don't treat scaffold as superseded — it's the source artifact, preserve as Promotions Log. Don't act on Phase 1+ atoms before dependencies land. Don't auto-close PROB-015 when atoms complete.
review_trigger: Q1 resolution favors ledger files inside Box (vs by reference)
Q2 resolution requires every Box to have local AGENTS.md
Q3 minimum viable box.json schema is meaningfully larger than current declarative form
Q4 resolution favors Page-Asset interpreter first (vs Atomizer Steward)
Q5 resolution mandates physical inbox/outbox folders now
Q6 resolution favors Page Box or Automation as first migration (vs Client Box)
Q7 UI tier visualization surfaces a new constraint on authority enforcement
Q8 'entering a Box' contract forces CLAUDE.md restructuring beyond anticipated
Phase 6 runtime experience shows fusion creates more friction than legibility
Regulatory/compliance requirement mandates strict separation of concerns
4h granularity rule produces atoms that can't be sized due to fused primitive's surface area

history.event: created. Foundation atom 2 of 3 in PROB-2026-04-30-015's decomposition pass. Triggered by Jake delivering Box Network Architecture scaffold (Codex co-author). Locks the architectural commitment that fuses Box + Ledger + Sub-agent into one mature primitive. Companion atoms: ATOM-2026-04-30-0045 (foundation, completed) + ATOM-2026-04-30-0047 (COMM handoff, follows). All 58 atoms in PROB-015 derive their authority from this DEC.

decisions_needing_review.question: What exact UI / process should isolate approval for risky moves?
decisions_needing_review.current_working_assumption: Risky moves become explicit approval cards requiring specific confirmation. Format and storage are not yet specified.
needs_decision_on: wording (what the card says about the risk)
approval logging (where the approval is recorded)
where approval lives (Client Box? _ledger/? dedicated approvals table?)
how approval attaches to future sends (signed token? reference ID? audit field?)
decisions_needing_review.suggested_timeline: When the approval surface gets concrete UI work, this becomes a real Decision.
